name: crimeStats
invariants: output.ok === false || output.result.clearanceRate === undefined || (Number.isFinite(output.result.clearanceRate) && output.result.clearanceRate >= 0 && output.result.clearanceRate <= 100)
output.ok === false || output.result.mostCommon === undefined || typeof output.result.mostCommon === 'string'
_note: Pure compute, reads artifact.data (LawEnforcementActionPanel.actStats: callMacro('crimeStats',{artifact:{data:parsed}}) → peel → artifact.data). clearanceRate = round(resolved/total*100) where resolved = i.resolved || i.status==='closed'; mostCommon = highest byType bucket; trend = total>100 ? 'high-volume' : 'normal'. EMPTY incidents returns the honest { message } prompt (NOT a fabricated 0% card) — the stats card only renders when totalIncidents is present, so clearanceRate is undefined on empty (honest). The CrimeStatsResult card renders trend/clearanceRate/totalIncidents/mostCommon/byType[{type,count}] EXACTLY. Non-array incidents → empty prompt, never NaN.
